get.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log entries. Supports filtering by action type (e.g., user.login, workspace.create), userId, date range (dateFrom/dateTo in ISO 8601), and free-text search. Date range is validated — dateTo must be after dateFrom. Scoped to workspace via X-Workspace-Id header (SYSOP sees all). Use the optional `fields` parameter to request only specific fields (e.g. `fields=id,action,userId,createdAt`). Returns { data: [...], page, limit, total }. Requires ADMIN role or higher.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return
parameters.name: fields

tokenInvalid.summary: Access token is malformed or expired

401.description: Authentication required or token is invalid/expired.

permissionDenied.summary: Role does not meet minimum requirement

post.description: Confirms a pending email change using the token delivered to the new address. On success, updates the account email, marks it verified, and revokes all user sessions (forcing re-login). Returns 400 with AUTH_EMAIL_CHANGE_TOKEN_INVALID if the token is expired or already used. Returns 409 if the target address was claimed by another account between request and confirmation. No authentication required.

value.message: Invalid or expired email change token
Email address is already in use
Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

get.description: Returns demo account credentials for quick testing and development. Only available in development mode and returns 403 in production. Demo accounts include all five RBAC roles: SYSOP, ADMIN, MANAGER, OPERATOR, VIEWER. All demo passwords are weak and should be changed in production. Response is cached for 1 hour.

data.warning: These demo accounts are for development and testing only. Never use these credentials in production.

value.message: Demo accounts are only available in development mode

403.description: Demo accounts disabled or in production mode.

post.description: Sends a password reset email to provided address. Always returns success to prevent email enumeration — even if the email does not exist. Requires SMTP to be configured. The reset token expires after a configurable duration.

200.description: Request accepted (always succeeds to prevent enumeration).

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Authenticates a user with username and password. On success without MFA, sets HttpOnly auth cookies (access token + refresh token) and returns user profile. When the user has MFA enabled, returns { mfaRequired: true, mfaToken } instead — the client must POST mfaToken + TOTP code to /auth/mfa/verify to complete login. Returns 401 with AUTH_INVALID_CREDENTIALS on bad credentials or expired password, AUTH_ACCOUNT_LOCKED after too many failed attempts.

200.description: Login successful (auth cookies set) OR MFA challenge issued (no cookies).

value.message: Account locked due to too many failed attempts

401.description: Invalid credentials or account locked.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

value.message: MFA is not configured on this server. Contact an administrator.

503.description: MFA is enabled for the account, but the server cannot issue a challenge token.

post.description: Logs out current user by clearing auth cookies and invalidating stored refresh token. Safe to call even without valid credentials — cookies are always cleared. Returns { data: null } on success.

get.description: Returns profile of currently authenticated user (id, username, email, role). Requires a valid access token cookie. Returns 401 with AUTH_TOKEN_MISSING, AUTH_TOKEN_INVALID, or AUTH_ACCOUNT_DELETED as applicable.

tokenInvalid.summary: Access token is malformed or expired

401.description: Authentication required or token is invalid/expired.

post.description: Disables MFA for the authenticated user. Requires a valid TOTP code to confirm.

value.message: Invalid code. MFA was not disabled.

401.description: Invalid verification code.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Regenerates recovery codes for the authenticated user. Requires a valid TOTP code. Previous recovery codes are invalidated.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Initiates TOTP MFA setup for the authenticated user. Requires the current password for step-up re-authentication. Returns a QR code URI (for scanning with an authenticator app) and the secret in base32. Recovery/backup codes are NOT returned here — they are emitted only after the user proves possession of the TOTP via POST /auth/mfa/verify-setup. MFA is NOT active until verified.

200.description: MFA setup initiated. Scan QR code with authenticator app.

401.description: Current password did not verify.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

get.description: Returns whether MFA is enabled for the authenticated user.

post.description: Completes MFA verification during login. Accepts the MFA challenge token (returned by /auth/login when MFA is required) and a TOTP code. On success, sets auth cookies and returns user profile.

value.message: Invalid MFA code. Please try again.
MFA challenge token is invalid or expired.

401.description: Invalid MFA code or challenge token.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Completes MFA verification using a one-time recovery code. The code is consumed on use and cannot be reused. Use when the authenticator app is unavailable.

200.description: Recovery code verified — auth cookies set.

401.description: Invalid recovery code or challenge token.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Verifies the initial TOTP code after MFA setup to confirm the user has correctly configured their authenticator app. On success, MFA is enabled and one-time backup/recovery codes are returned exactly once for the user to save.

200.description: MFA verified and enabled; backup codes returned once.

value.message: Invalid verification code. Please try again.

401.description: Invalid verification code.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

get.description: Returns the list of OAuth providers enabled in the server configuration (e.g., GitHub, Google). No authentication required. Returns { data: { providers: string[] } }.

200.description: List of enabled OAuth provider names.

get.description: Initiates an OAuth login flow by redirecting the user to the specified provider authorization URL. Supported providers depend on the server config. Returns 404 if the provider is not enabled or does not exist.

Handles the OAuth callback after the user authorizes with an external provider. Exchanges the authorization code for tokens, creates or links the user account, sets auth cookies, and redirects to /dashboard. Returns 400 with AUTH_OAUTH_FAILED if the code is missing or the exchange fails.

200.description: OAuth login successful — cookies set, redirecting.

value.message: Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

429.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded.

post.description: Rotates access/refresh token pair using refresh cookie. Issues new tokens and updates cookies. Implements refresh-token rotation — reuse of a previously rotated token revokes all sessions for user (AUTH_TOKEN_REVOKED). Also returns AUTH_ACCOUNT_DELETED if account was removed, or AUTH_ACCOUNT_LOCKED if account is locked due to failed attempts.

tokenRevoked.summary: Reuse of rotated refresh token detected

401.description: Refresh token missing, invalid, revoked, or account deleted.

accountLocked.summary: Account locked due to failed attempts

value.message: Account locked due to too many failed attempts

409.description: Concurrent refresh detected — retry the request.

post.summary: Refresh access token using refresh cookie

post.description: Registers a new user account with VIEWER role. Username must be 2-50 alphanumeric characters, password 8-128 characters with uppercase, lowercase, and a digit, and email must be valid. The new user is automatically added to the default workspace as VIEWER. A verification email is sent if SMTP is configured. Returns 400 for weak passwords or mismatched confirmation.

data.message: Registration successful. Please check your email.

value.message: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter
Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 60 seconds.

get.description: Returns whether self-registration is currently enabled. This is a public endpoint — no authentication required.

post.description: Completes a password reset using the token from the reset email. The new password must be 8-128 characters. Returns 400 with AUTH_RESET_TOKEN_INVALID if token is expired or already used. On success, the old refresh token is invalidated and the user must log in again.

post.description: Stop impersonating and restore the original SYSOP session. Revokes the impersonation access token and restores the stashed original token.

put.description: Updates authenticated user's own profile (username only). Email changes must go through POST /users/me/email-change which requires password re-authentication and an out-of-band confirmation link delivered to the new address. Username must be 2-50 alphanumeric characters (plus _ . -). Returns 409 if new username is already taken.

401.description: Authentication required or token is invalid/expired.

post.description: Initiates an email change for the authenticated user. Requires the current password for step-up re-authentication. Sends a one-time confirmation link to the NEW address and an informational notification to the OLD address. The user's account email is NOT changed until the confirmation link is clicked via POST /auth/confirm-email-change. Returns 401 if the current password is incorrect, 409 if the new email is already in use.

post.description: Start impersonating another user (SYSOP only). Issues a new access token for the target user and stashes the SYSOP's original token in a separate HttpOnly cookie. Rejects self-impersonation and re-impersonation attempts.

Allows an admin to reset another user's password in two modes: 'set' (directly provide a new password) or 'email' (send a self-service reset token). Target users are forced through /change-password on next login. SYSOP users can only be reset by other SYSOPs. Requires ADMIN role or higher.
